message:
feat(tui): Dramatically improve UI look and feel

Enhanced theme system with extended HUD colors:
- Added 18 new color properties to HudTheme interface
- backgroundAlt, textMuted, borderLight for subtle variations
- info color for informational elements
- geneForward/geneReverse/geneHighlight for strand visualization
- gradientLow/Mid/High for sparkline color gradients
- kmerNormal/kmerAnomaly for k-mer visualization
- shadow/highlight for visual effects
- Updated all 5 themes (Classic, Ocean, Matrix, Sunset, Forest)

Redesigned Header component:
- Added decorative elements and experience level indicator
- Better visual hierarchy with bullet points and separators
- Mode toggle buttons with background highlighting
- Compact yet informative phage metadata display

Improved Footer with color-coded hints:
- Navigation hints in blue (info)
- View controls in green (success)
- Analysis overlays in yellow (warning)
- Overlay controls in accent color
- Experience level badge with visual distinction

Enhanced PhageList:
- Rounded borders for consistency
- Host abbreviations and genome size display
- Better selection highlighting with background
- Improved scroll indicators

New Sparkline component:
- Reusable gradient-colored sparklines
- Support for bars, braille, and blocks styles
- ProgressBar and DualBar helper components
- Configurable min/max and highlight positions

Improved GeneMap with strand coloring:
- Different colors for forward (+) and reverse (-) strands
- Overlap detection with warning color
- Position percentage and current gene info
- K-mer anomaly strip with gradient coloring

Polished overlay components:
- GCOverlay with GC/AT ratio bar and better layout
- SearchOverlay with result count and improved styling
- HelpOverlay with color-coded sections by category
- Consistent rounded borders and separators

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"phage_explorer-cgh","title":"Layer 3: Plaque Growth Cellular Automata","description":"Cellular automaton simulating phage plaque formation on bacterial lawns. Input phage parameters (burst size, latent period, lysogeny probability, diffusion rate) and watch virtual plaques grow in real-time. Models reaction-diffusion: bacteria grow, phages infect, cells lyse after latent period releasing burst, phages diffuse. Explains clear vs turbid plaques, size determinants. Novelty: 9/10, Pedagogical: 9/10, Cool Factor: 10/10.","notes":"## PLAQUE MORPHOLOGY CELLULAR AUTOMATA

**Comprehensive Feature Specification from Roadmap**

### Concept
Simulate phage plaque formation using cellular automaton, connecting genotype (molecular parameters) to phenotype (observable plaque morphology).

**Biology of Plaque Formation**:
- Bacteria grow as lawn on solid media
- Single phage infects one bacterium
- After latent period, cell lyses releasing burst_size new phages
- Phages diffuse outward, infect neighbors
- Process repeats, creating expanding dead zone (plaque)

**What Determines Morphology**:
- Clear plaques: Virulent phages that always lyse
- Turbid plaques: Temperate phages, lysogens survive in center
- Large plaques: Long latent period (more diffusion time before lysis)
- Small plaques: Short latent period (lysis before diffusion)
- Halo plaques: Phages with depolymerase activity

### Mathematical Foundations

**Reaction-Diffusion Model**:
State variables at (x,y,t):
- B(x,y,t) = bacteria density [0,1]
- I(x,y,t) = infected cell count
- P(x,y,t) = free phage particles

Equations:
dB/dt = r*B*(1-B) - k*B*P        [growth - infection]
dI/dt = k*B*P - (1/tau)*I        [infection - lysis]
dP/dt = D*grad^2(P) + b*(1/tau)*I - delta*P  [diffusion + burst - decay]

Parameters: r=growth, k=adsorption, tau=latent period, b=burst size, D=diffusion, delta=decay

**Cellular Automaton Simplification**:
Each cell per tick:
- BACTERIA: if adjacent PHAGE, become INFECTED with prob p; else stay/divide
- INFECTED: age++; if age >= latent_period: LYSED, spawn burst_size phages nearby
- LYSED: stay dead
- LYSOGEN: immune, can grow (temperate phages)

**Plaque Size Prediction**:
R ~ sqrt(4 * D * T * efficiency)
Plaque area proportional to (burst_size * latent_period * D) / adsorption

### Implementation

**Core Interfaces**:
```typescript
type CellState = 'empty' | 'bacteria' | 'infected' | 'lysed' | 'lysogen';

interface PlaqueSimConfig {
  gridSize: number;           // NxN grid
  burstSize: number;          // Phages per lysis
  latentPeriod: number;       // Ticks until lysis
  diffusionRate: number;      // Phage movement
  adsorptionProb: number;     // Infection probability
  lysogenyProb: number;       // Lysogen probability (temperate)
  bacteriaGrowthRate: number; // Division probability
}

interface SimulationCell {
  state: CellState;
  phageCount: number;
  infectionAge: number;
}
```

**initializePlaqueSim()**: Create grid of bacteria, seed single phage at center

**simulateTick()**: 
- Process each cell by state
- Handle infection, lysis, phage release
- Diffuse phages to neighbors
- Update grid

**distributePhages()**: Spread burst_size phages in random directions within 3-cell radius

**renderPlaque()**: Convert cell states to ASCII: bacteria=block, infected=dark shade, lysed=space, lysogen=light shade

### TUI Visualization

```
╭───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  PLAQUE MORPHOLOGY SIMULATOR: Lambda Phage                               │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                           │
│  Parameters:         Time: 00:45    Generation: 3                        │
│  ├─ Burst size: 100                                                       │
│  ├─ Latent period: 45 min                                                 │
│  ├─ Lysogeny: 50%                                                         │
│  └─ Diffusion: Medium                                                     │
│                                                                           │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████│  │
│  │████████████████████████████▓▓▓██████████████████████████████████████│  │
│  │██████████████████████████▓▓   ▓▓████████████████████████████████████│  │
│  │████████████████████████▓▓  ▒▒  ▓▓██████████████████████████████████│  │
│  │███████████████████████▓  ▒    ▒  ▓█████████████████████████████████│  │
│  │██████████████████████▓ ▒  ▒▒▒  ▒ ▓█████████████████████████████████│  │
│  │█████████████████████▓ ▒ ▒     ▒ ▒ ▓████████████████████████████████│  │
│  │█████████████████████▓ ▒   ▒▒▒  ▒  ▓████████████████████████████████│  │
│  │██████████████████████▓ ▒ ▒   ▒ ▒ ▓█████████████████████████████████│  │
│  │███████████████████████▓  ▒▒▒▒▒  ▓██████████████████████████████████│  │
│  │████████████████████████▓▓     ▓▓███████████████████████████████████│  │
│  │██████████████████████████▓▓▓▓▓█████████████████████████████████████│  │
│  │█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████│  │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                           │
│  Legend: █ Bacteria | ▓ Infected | (space) Lysed | ▒ Lysogen             │
│                                                                           │
│  Morphology: TURBID (lysogens surviving in center)                       │
│  Predicted size: Medium (2.1 mm at 24h)                                  │
│                                                                           │
│  [Space] Pause  [B/L/D] Adjust params  [R] Reset  [V] Virulent  [ESC]    │
╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Assessment
- Novelty: 9/10 - Cellular automata for plaques exists in research but never in phage tools, real-time TUI unique
- Pedagogical: 9/10 - Teaches phage lifecycle, population dynamics, mathematical biology, emergence
- Cool Factor: 10/10 - Essentially a video game, watching infection spread is addictive

 {"id":"phage_explorer-cu3","title":"Advanced: Metagenomic Co-Occurrence & Niche Profiler","description":"Infer ecological niches and co-occurring taxa from metagenomic abundance tables. SparCC/FlashWeave-like compositional correlations + NMF for latent niche vectors. Co-occurrence network colored by niches, habitat filters, significance thresholds. Adds real-world context to host predictions. Novelty: Medium-high, Pedagogical: High (compositional stats, niche ecology).","notes":"## METAGENOMIC CO-OCCURRENCE & ECOLOGICAL NICHE PROFILER

### Concept
Infer ecological niches and co-occurring taxa from metagenomic abundance tables; derive niche vectors and networks.

### How to Build
- **Input**: BIOM/TSV + sample metadata
- **Correlations**: SparCC/FlashWeave-like compositional inference in Rust+WASM (ndarray + rayon)
- **Niche factors**: NMF/PMF in TS for latent "niche vectors"
- **Metadata mapping**: Attach habitats/hosts to factors; cache per phage
- **UI**: Co-occurrence network colored by niches; stacked niche bars per phage; filters by habitat

### Why It's Good
Adds real-world context to host predictions; complements CRISPR/prophage evidence.

### Implementation Stack
Rust+WASM for correlations; TS NMF; SQLite cache of niche vectors; BIOM parser in TS.

### TUI Visualization
Network with niche colors; press 'n' to repaint bars; tooltips show top co-occurring taxa/habitats; toggle significance threshold live.

### Files
- packages/core/src/analysis/metagenomic-niche.ts
- packages/tui/src/components/NicheNetworkView.tsx","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:51:11.070369-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T00:15:39.340902-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-cu3","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-54g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:56:51.160196-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-d2p","title":"Advanced: Regulatory Signal Constellations","description":"Scan promoters/terminators/RBS/operators; render co-occurring motifs as constellations. PWMs + co-occurrence graphs. Evidence score combining spacing, orientation, strength into operon-likelihood. Starfield strip above genes; motifs as glyphs; toggle inferred operons. Novelty: Medium, Pedagogical: High.","notes":"## REGULATORY SIGNAL CONSTELLATIONS

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Scan promoters/terminators/RBS/operators and render co-occurring regulatory motifs as 'constellations' to reveal operons and control logic.

### How to Build

**Motif Scans:**
- PWMs for sigma factors (σ70, σ32, σ54, σS)
- Rho-independent terminator detection (stem-loop + poly-U)
- RBS (Shine-Dalgarno) scoring
- Operator palindrome detection
- Run in TS (fast) or Rust+WASM for batch

**Co-occurrence Graph:**
- Build small graph of motifs per region
- Detect motif 'motifs' (regulatory patterns):
  - Promoter + operator + terminator spacing
  - Dual promoters (divergent)
  - Antitermination signals

**Evidence Score:**
- Combine spacing, orientation, and strength
- Operon-likelihood per gene cluster
- Regulatory complexity score

**Link to Genes:**
- Tie regulatory regions to downstream ORFs
- Annotate likely operons and regulons

### TUI Features
- Starfield strip above genes (motifs as glyphs)
- Edges show spacing relationships
- Hover shows PWM score for each motif
- Press 'o' to toggle inferred operon highlighting
- ORF blocks colored by regulatory context
- Legend showing motif glyph meanings

### Why It's Good
- Surfaces regulatory architecture beyond genes
- Shows how multiple motifs compose control logic
- Aids understanding of temporal expression
- Spacing and phase matter for function

### Implementation Stack
- TS PWM scanning
- Optional Rust+WASM for speed on large genomes
- SQLite motif hit cache
- Ink starfield rendering with braille and symbols

-{"id":"phage_explorer-dr7","title":"Advanced: Amino-Acid Property Phase Portraits","description":"Slide across proteins computing property vectors (hydropathy, charge, aromaticity, disorder). Project trajectories via PCA/UMAP. Compare homologous proteins between phages. ASCII scatter/trajectory; cursor shows window position; color by property dominance. Novelty: Medium-high, Pedagogical: High.","notes":"## AMINO-ACID PROPERTY PHASE PORTRAITS

**Full TypeScript Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Create compositional landscape visualizations by computing property trajectories along proteins and projecting to 2D phase portraits that reveal domain structure.

### Mathematical Foundations

**Window Property Vector:**
```
P(w) = [H(w), Q(w), A(w), F(w), D(w)]

- H(w): Mean hydropathy (Kyte-Doolittle)
- Q(w): Net charge ((K+R) - (D+E)) / W
- A(w): Aromaticity (F+W+Y) / W
- F(w): Flexibility (B-factor proxy)
- D(w): Disorder propensity
```

**PCA Projection:**
```
1. Center: P_centered = P - mean(P)
2. Covariance: C = P^T × P / (n-1)
3. Power iteration for eigenvectors
4. Project: P_2D = P_centered × [v1, v2]
```

**Fréchet Distance:**
```
d_F(A, B) = min_{α,β} max_t ||A(α(t)) - B(β(t))||
```

### Complete TypeScript Implementation

- `PropertyVector` (hydropathy, charge, aromaticity, flexibility, disorder)
- `ProteinWindow` with properties and 2D projection
- `ProteinTrajectory` with windows and detected domains
- `DetectedDomain` (name, dominant property, centroid)
- `HYDROPATHY` Kyte-Doolittle scale (I:4.5 to R:-4.5)
- `FLEXIBILITY` B-factor proxy scale
- `DISORDER_PROPENSITY` IUPred-like scale
- `computePropertyVector()` per window
- `computeTrajectory()` sliding window (30 aa, step 5)
- `matrixMean()`, `centerMatrix()`, `computeCovariance()`
- `powerIteration()` for top 2 eigenvectors with deflation
- `projectToPCA()` apply projection
- `detectDomains()` clustering by dominant property
- `getDominantProperty()` with normalization
- `analyzePhasePortraits()` main pipeline

### Braille Rendering
- `BRAILLE_BASE = 0x2800`
- `BRAILLE_DOTS[4][2]` dot encoding
- `renderPhasePortrait()` trajectory as braille scatter
- `renderDomainBar()` with domain-specific characters:
  - ▓ Hydrophobic core
  - ± Charged region
  - ◆ Aromatic cluster
  - ~ Flexible linker
  - ? Disordered region

### TUI Features
- Braille scatter plot (40×12 grid)
- N-term → C-term trajectory path
- PC loadings display (which properties drive each axis)
- Explained variance percentages
- Domain map bar under trajectory
- Domain annotations with boundaries
- [←/→] Navigate proteins, [C] Compare, [D] Jump to domain

 {"id":"phage_explorer-dx3","title":"Foundation: UI Design System & Visual Language","description":"## UI Design System & Visual Language

Establishes coherent visual language across all 31 features to maintain consistency.

### Color Semantics
- Green (#22c55e): Good, conserved, stable, similar
- Yellow (#eab308): Caution, moderate, neutral
- Red (#ef4444): Warning, divergent, unstable, different
- Blue (#3b82f6): Informational, cool metrics
- Purple (#a855f7): Special, unique, notable
- Gray (#6b7280): Inactive, background, reference

### Graph/Sparkline Standards
- Height: 3-5 character rows for overlays
- Width: Full terminal width
- Scale: Auto-normalize, show min/max in labels
- Style: Braille dots for high resolution

### Panel/Modal Standards
- Border: Single line box drawing
- Header: Bold, centered, with close hint
- Padding: 1 character on all sides
- Max width: 80 characters (or terminal width - 4)

This is the foundational design system that all features must follow.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:35:34.98277-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-06T23:58:14.201697-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-06T23:58:14.201697-05:00"}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-dxv","title":"Advanced: Pan-Phage Graph Pangenome & Structural Variant Cards","description":"Build graph pangenome over bundled phages via Rust petgraph + minimap2 bindings. Compress bubbles into variant records (SNP/indel/structural). Per-bubble metadata in SQLite. Mini ASCII graph ribbon with bubbles; press enter for variant card showing diff stats, donor lineage bar, overlapped genes. Shows modular mosaicism and reassortment. Novelty: High.","notes":"## PAN-PHAGE GRAPH PANGENOME & STRUCTURAL VARIANT CARDS

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Build a graph pangenome over all bundled phages to expose conserved cores, accessory modules, recombination breakpoints, and mosaicism. Per-variant 'cards' show local diff, donor lineage hints, and functional impact.

### How to Build

**Graph Construction:**
- Rust (petgraph + rayon) for rGFA-like graph
- Align genomes with minimap2 bindings or rust-bio aligners
- Compress bubbles into variant records (SNP/indel/structural)

**Annotation Linkage:**
- Map genes, AMGs, defense/anti-defense loci onto graph nodes
- Tag breakpoints with recombination evidence

**Variant Cards:**
- Per-bubble metadata in SQLite:
  - Size, gene overlap, GC shift, donor similarity
- Query via WASM-compiled graph summaries

### TUI Features
- Mini ASCII graph ribbon with bubbles highlighted
- Press enter to pop 'variant card':
  - Diff stats (size, gene count)
  - Donor lineage bar
  - Overlapped genes list
- Jump sequence grid to variant locus
- Filter by: size, function, donor
- Core vs accessory genome coloring

### Why It's Good
- Shows modular mosaicism and reassortment
- Surfaces hotspots for host range or defense evasion
- Reveals conserved backbone vs exchanged islands
- Goes beyond linear diffs to real evolutionary story

### Implementation Stack
- Rust+WASM graph builder (petgraph, rust-bio)
- minimap2 binding for alignments
- SQLite for variant cards
- TS/Ink UI for bubble strips and drilldowns

 {"id":"phage_explorer-ea3","title":"Foundation: Depth Layers UI Philosophy & Keybinding Conventions","description":"## Depth Layers UI Philosophy & Keybinding Conventions

### Core Philosophy

Phage Explorer must serve two audiences:
1. **Newcomers** who need simplicity and discoverability
2. **Power users** who need speed and comprehensive access

The solution: **Depth Layers** — a progressive disclosure system where features are organized by access frequency and complexity.

### The Five Layers

```
Layer 0: Sacred Surface (12 core controls)
  ↓ Always visible, never change
Layer 1: Quick Overlays (5 single-key toggles)
  ↓ One keypress, instant visual augmentation
Layer 2: Analysis Menu (A key)
  ↓ Categorized selection, ~15 analyses
Layer 3: Simulation Hub (Shift+S)
  ↓ Interactive simulations, 5 tools
Layer 4: Command Palette (: or Ctrl+P)
  ↓ Fuzzy search everything, 31+ features
```

### Layer 0: Sacred Surface (NEVER CHANGE)

These 12 controls are the foundation. New users learn these first.

```
NAVIGATION:
  ↑/↓       Navigate phages
  ←/→       Scroll sequence
  PgUp/PgDn Fast scroll
  Home/End  Jump to start/end

VIEW CONTROLS:
  N/C/Space Toggle DNA/AA view
  F         Cycle reading frame (AA mode)
  T         Cycle theme
  D         Toggle diff mode
  M         Toggle 3D model

META:
  ?         Help overlay
  S or /    Search phages
  Q         Quit
```

**RULE**: These keys MUST remain unchanged forever. Any new feature uses other keys.

### Layer 1: Quick Overlays

Single-key toggles for visual augmentation. These overlay information on the main sequence view.

```
G - GC Skew curve (replication origin)
X - Complexity sparkline (HGT regions)
B - Bendability track (packaging signals)
P - Promoter/RBS annotations
R - Repeat/palindrome markers
```

**Design Principle**: One key = one overlay. Toggle on/off. Max 3 active overlays.

**Precomputation**: All overlay data computed on phage selection, not on key press. This makes overlays feel instant.

### Layer 2: Analysis Menu

Press `A` to open categorized analysis menu:

```
╭─────────── Analysis Menu ───────────╮
│                                      │
│  WHOLE GENOME                        │
│  [1] GC Skew Analysis                │
│  [2] Chaos Game Representation       │
│  [3] Hilbert Curve Atlas             │
│  [4] Self-Homology Dot Plot          │
│  [5] Tetranucleotide PCA             │
│                                      │
│  EVOLUTION                           │
│  [6] dN/dS Selection Pressure        │
│  [7] Recombination Hotspots          │
│  [8] Protein Domain Chord Plot       │
│                                      │
│  FUNCTION                            │
│  [9] Codon Adaptation Dashboard      │
│  [a] Host Range Predictor            │
│  [b] Arms Race Scanner               │
│  ...                                 │
│                                      │
│  [/] Search   [ESC] Cancel           │
╰──────────────────────────────────────╯
```

**Design Principle**: Organized by category, numbered shortcuts, fuzzy search.

### Layer 3: Simulation Hub

Press `Shift+S` to open interactive simulation selector:

```
╭────────── Simulation Hub ───────────╮
│                                      │
│  INTERACTIVE SIMULATIONS             │
│                                      │
│  [1] Lysogeny Decision Circuit       │
│      ODE model of lysis/lysogeny     │
│      switch with MOI and UV params   │
│                                      │
│  [2] Ribosome Traffic Jam            │
│      Translation particle simulation │
│      showing codon effects           │
│                                      │
│  [3] Plaque Growth Automata          │
│      2D cellular automaton of        │
│      plaque formation                │
│                                      │
│  [4] Temporal Evolution Replay       │
│      Animated phylogenetic time-     │
│      lapse with mutation events      │
│                                      │
│  [5] Packaging Motor Pressure        │
│      60 atm inside the capsid!       │
│                                      │
│  [ESC] Cancel                        │
╰──────────────────────────────────────╯
```

**Design Principle**: Full-screen takeover, common controls (Space=pause, R=reset, arrows=speed).

### Layer 4: Command Palette

Press `:` or `Ctrl+P` for fuzzy search of ALL features:

```
╭────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ :gc skew                                                     [ESC] │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ▶ GC Skew Replication Origin        [G]       Overlay             │
│   Chaos Game Representation         [A→2]     Analysis            │
│   Genomic Signature PCA             [A→5]     Analysis            │
│   GC Content Display                [built-in]                     │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

**Design Principle**: Type anything, find everything. Show shortcut paths.

### Keystroke Budget

```
LAYER 0 (reserved, untouchable):
  ↑↓←→ PgUp PgDn Home End
  N C Space F T D M ? S / Q

LAYER 1 (quick overlays):
  G X B P R  (5 keys)

LAYER 2 (menu trigger):
  A

LAYER 3 (simulation trigger):
  Shift+S

LAYER 4 (command palette):
  : or Ctrl+P

LAYER 2+ FEATURES (accessed via menu):
  W (comparison - already exists)
  K (AA key - already exists)
  
RESERVED FOR FUTURE:
  E I O U Y  (vowels for mnemonic features)
  H J L Z    (vim-like navigation if needed)
  1-9        (quick phage selection?)
```

### Risk Mitigation Strategies

**Risk: Feature bloat slows main loop**
- Mitigation: Lazy-load analysis components (`React.lazy`)
- Mitigation: Precompute in background on phage selection
- Mitigation: Cache aggressively in SQLite

**Risk: Users get lost in menus**
- Mitigation: ESC always returns to main view
- Mitigation: Breadcrumb trail in status bar
- Mitigation: "Back" action in every modal

**Risk: Small terminal breaks layouts**
- Mitigation: Detect terminal size, hide overlays if too small
- Mitigation: Stack overlays vertically if narrow
- Mitigation: Minimum viable mode = Layer 0 only

**Risk: Keybinding conflicts**
- Mitigation: Layer 0 = unmodified lowercase (sacred)
- Mitigation: Layer 1 = specific lowercase (G, X, B, P, R)
- Mitigation: Layer 2/3 = Shift+letter or menu selection
- Mitigation: Layer 4 = `:` prefix (command mode)

### Accessibility Metrics

```
Total keystrokes to any feature: 1-3
Visible complexity for new users: 12 controls
Available power for experts: 31+ features
```

### Implementation Checklist

- [ ] Document Layer 0 keys in help overlay
- [ ] Implement overlay manager (max 3 active)
- [ ] Create ModalMenu component for Layer 2/3
- [ ] Build CommandRegistry singleton
- [ ] Implement fuzzy search for palette
- [ ] Add breadcrumb to status bar
- [ ] Precompute overlay data on phage selection
- [ ] Test on 80x24 minimum terminal

### This Epic Blocks
